rules.fix: Add a <title> tag to your page's <head> section
Expand your title to at least 30 characters — short titles waste ranking potential
Shorten your title to under 60 characters to prevent truncation in search results
Include your primary target keyword in the title tag

rules.description: Keyword appears in the first half of the title
rules.fix: Move your target keyword closer to the beginning of the title for stronger ranking signals
Remove repeated words from your title — keyword stuffing hurts rankings

rules.description: Title includes brand or site name
rules.fix: Consider appending your brand name (e.g., '| YourBrand') for brand recognition

rules.description: Title is not a generic default
rules.fix: Replace the generic default title with a descriptive, keyword-rich title
Add a <meta name='description'> tag — this directly controls your search snippet

rules.description: Meta description is at least 120 characters
rules.fix: Expand your meta description to at least 120 characters to use the full snippet space
Trim your meta description to under 160 characters to prevent truncation

rules.description: Target keyword appears in meta description
rules.fix: Include your target keyword in the meta description — it gets bolded in search results
Add a call-to-action (e.g., 'Learn more', 'Get started today') to improve click-through rate

rules.description: Meta description is not identical to title
rules.fix: Write a unique meta description that expands on (not duplicates) your title tag
Add an <h1> tag — every page needs exactly one primary heading

rules.description: Page has exactly one H1 tag
rules.fix: Reduce to a single <h1> tag — multiple H1s dilute heading hierarchy signals
Include your primary keyword in the H1 heading
Adjust your H1 length to 20-70 characters for optimal readability and SEO

rules.description: Headings follow proper hierarchy (H1 > H2 > H3)
rules.fix: Fix heading hierarchy — don't skip levels (e.g., H1 directly to H3). Use H2 under H1, H3 under H2

rules.description: Page has at least 3 headings for content structure
rules.fix: Add more subheadings (H2, H3) to break up content — improves readability and SEO
Remove or fill empty heading tags — empty headings confuse crawlers
Add descriptive alt text to all images — critical for accessibility and image search

rules.description: Alt text is descriptive (not just 'image' or 'photo')
rules.fix: Replace generic alt text with descriptive text that explains the image content

rules.description: At least one image alt includes target keyword
rules.fix: Include your target keyword naturally in at least one image alt attribute
Keep image alt text concise — under 125 characters for screen reader compatibility
Add internal links to other pages on your site — essential for crawling and page authority

rules.description: Page has external links to authoritative sources
rules.fix: Add links to authoritative external sources — shows Google your content is well-researched

rules.description: Internal to external link ratio is reasonable (2:1 to 5:1)
rules.fix: Adjust your internal/external link ratio — aim for 2-5 internal links per external link
Fix or remove broken links — they hurt user experience and waste crawl budget

rules.description: Links use descriptive anchor text (not 'click here')
rules.fix: Replace generic anchor text ('click here') with descriptive text that includes relevant keywords
Use a clean, readable URL structure with hyphens between words

rules.description: URL doesn't have excessive query parameters
rules.fix: Minimize URL parameters — clean URLs rank better and are more shareable

rules.description: URL uses hyphens instead of underscores
rules.fix: Replace underscores with hyphens in URLs — Google treats hyphens as word separators
Shorten your URL path — shorter URLs tend to rank better and are more user-friendly
Use lowercase URLs to avoid duplicate content issues — URLs are case-sensitive
Add JSON-LD structured data markup — it enables rich snippets and helps AI understand your content

rules.description: Schema type is appropriate for content
rules.fix: Use the correct Schema.org type for your content (Article, Product, FAQ, HowTo, etc.)

rules.description: Schema markup includes all recommended properties
rules.fix: Add missing recommended properties to your schema markup for maximum rich snippet coverage
Add a <link rel='canonical'> tag to prevent duplicate content issues
Add Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image) for better social media sharing
Add Twitter Card meta tags for optimized Twitter/X sharing previews

rules.description: Page has a mobile viewport meta tag
rules.fix: Add <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1'> for mobile compatibility

rules.description: HTML tag has a lang attribute
rules.fix: Add lang attribute to your <html> tag (e.g., <html lang='en'>) for accessibility and SEO
Add <meta charset='UTF-8'> in your <head> for proper character rendering
Add a favicon — it appears in browser tabs and search results, improving brand recognition
Migrate to HTTPS immediately — Google penalizes non-secure sites and browsers show warnings
Create a robots.txt file to guide search engine crawling
Create and submit a sitemap.xml to help search engines discover all your pages

rules.description: Page has at least 300 words of content
rules.fix: Add more content — pages with under 300 words are considered 'thin content' by Google

rules.description: Page has 1000+ words for comprehensive coverage
rules.fix: Consider expanding to 1000+ words — longer, comprehensive content ranks higher for most queries
Adjust keyword density to 1-3% — too low means weak relevance signals, too high means keyword stuffing

rules.description: Page has date metadata indicating freshness
rules.fix: Add datePublished and dateModified metadata — search engines and AI prefer fresh content

rules.description: Content uses short paragraphs and varied sentence length
rules.fix: Break up long paragraphs and vary sentence length for better readability
